titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 573

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=888 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.464527+00 | translation moa=889 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.474939+00

p.cl: D . dei gratia Rex Scottorum . Omnibus Sanctę ęcclesię fidelibus presentibus et futuris  salutem .

Sciatis me concessisse Eccelsię Sancte Marię . et sancti
Cuthberti de Coldingham . et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus Donum quod Gospatricus frater Dolfini dedit predictę ęcclesię
et monachis . scilicet Ederham . et Nesbitam in perpetuam elemosinam . cum ecclesiis . et aquis . et pratis . et pascuis . et molen
dinis . et omnibus aliis locis . et cum eisdem Rectis diuisis . quibus eas tenuit die qua fuit uiuus et mortuus . liberas et
quietas . ab omni seruitio . et omni consuetudine . exceptis triginta solidos . quos prefati monachi dabunt filio eius
Gospatrico . et heredibus suis post eum pro conredio Regis . unoquoque anno ad festum sancti Martini . et excepto exer
citu Regis . unde monachi erunt attendentes ipsi Regi . et ipse Gospatricus de exercitu erit quietus inper
petuum .

Presentibus testibus . Roberto episcopo Sancti Andree . Iohanne Glasguensi episcopo . herberto abbate de Rochesburc . Ricardo abbate
de Mailros . Ædwardo Cancellario . Thor archidiacono . Hugone constabulario . Herberto Camerario . Randulf’ de Sules . Valter de Rid’
Roberto de Burneuill’ . Horm filio Malcolm . Normanno vicecomite de Berwic . Roberto filio Widonis vicecomite de Rochesburc Sain
sacerdote de fiswic’ . Willelmo de Lambertun . aldano de prenregest’ .

Apud Coldingham . In Die inuentionis sancte
crucis . anno . m . c . xlvii [written lxvii] . ab in Carnatione domini . videlicet illo anno in quo Rex francie et multi christiani per
rexerunt ierusalem .
D(avid), by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all Holy Church's faithful, present and to come, greeting.

Know me to have allowed to the church of St Mary and St Cuthbert at Coldingham and to the monks serving God there the gift which Cospatric brother of Dolphin gave to the aforesaid church and monks, that is to say, Edrom and Nisbet in perpetual alms, with churches, waters, meadows, pastures, mills, and all other places, and with the same correct boundaries by which he held them on the day on which he was alive and dead, free and quit from every service, and every custom, except for thirty shillings which the said monks shall give to his son Cospatric and his heirs after him in return for the king's corrody each year at the feast of St Martin, and excepting the king's army service, for which the monks shall be responsible to the king himself; and Cospatric shall be quit in respect of army service for ever.

Present as witnesses: Robert the bishop of St Andrews, John the bishop of Glasgow, Herbert the abbot of Roxburgh, Richard the abbot of Melrose, Edward the chancellor, Thor the archdeacon, Hugh the constable, Herbert the chamberlain, Ralf de Soules, Walter of Ryedale, Robert of Bournville, Orm son of Malcolm, Norman sheriff of Berwick, Robert son of Guy, the sheriff of Roxburgh, Swain the priest of Fishwick, William of Lamberton, Haldane of Prenderguest.

At Coldingham on the day of the Invention of the Holy Cross, in the year 1147 since the incarnation of the Lord, that is, the year in which the king of France and many Christiants made their way to Jerusalem.
